title: Clean up a branch with interactive rebase
description: A procedure to reorder, squash, and reword commits on a private branch before review so the history reads as a deliberate sequence.
body: Use when a private feature branch has noisy work-in-progress commits before opening it for review. Do not run this on commits others have already based work on. Steps: run git rebase -i against the base; mark commits to squash or fixup and reword messages; verify each retained commit still builds; force-with-lease only if the branch was already pushed. Validation signals include a tidy linear history and a green build at the final commit. Risk boundary: interactive rebase rewrites hashes, so confine it to unpublished work or confirm with collaborators first.
